---
title: UniProt
alias: &ALIAS uniprot
href: https://www.uniprot.org/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

UniProt is the world's leading high-quality, comprehensive, and freely accessible resource of protein sequence and functional information, curated by the UniProt Consortium.

## Key Features

- Swiss-Prot provides manually curated protein records with functional annotations and literature support
- TrEMBL provides computationally annotated records for all translated sequences in INSDC databases
- Links proteins to GO terms, pathways, domains (Pfam/InterPro), variants, and 3D structures
- Provides programmatic access via UniProt REST API and SPARQL endpoint
- Integrates with nearly all major biological databases through cross-reference linking

## Use Cases in Research

- Retrieving functional annotations for AMR-related proteins such as beta-lactamases and efflux pumps
- Mapping pathogen proteomes to known functions, domains, and interaction partners
- Generating protein feature matrices for AI-driven AMR gene function prediction
- Identifying conserved functional domains in novel resistance proteins from metagenomic data
- Linking sequence variants in microbial proteins to known resistance or virulence phenotypes

## Getting Started

Visit [UniProt](https://www.uniprot.org/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
